title: Go Mod and Modules
content:
### Go Modules

Go modules are the standard dependency management system.

### Initializing a Module

```bash
go mod init example.com/myproject
```

This creates a `go.mod` file:

```
module example.com/myproject

go 1.22
```

### Adding Dependencies

```bash
go get github.com/gorilla/mux@v1.8.0
```

### go.mod File

```
module github.com/user/myapp

go 1.22

require (
    github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.0
    golang.org/x/text v0.14.0
)
```

### go.sum File

The `go.sum` file contains cryptographic checksums for dependency verification:

```
github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.0 h1:Tu10...
github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.0/go.mod h1:...
```

### Common go mod Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `go mod init` | Initialize new module |
| `go mod tidy` | Add missing, remove unused deps |
| `go mod download` | Download all dependencies |
| `go mod verify` | Verify dependencies |
| `go mod why` | Explain why a dependency is needed |
| `go mod graph` | Print dependency graph |

### Upgrading Dependencies

```bash
go get -u                   # update all direct deps
go get -u ./...            # update all deps (including indirect)
go get github.com/foo@v1.2.3  # specific version
go get github.com/foo@latest  # latest version
```
### Vendor Directory

```bash
go mod vendor  # creates vendor/ directory with all deps
```

Build with vendored dependencies:

```bash
go build -mod=vendor
```

### Replacing Dependencies

Useful for local development:

```
replace github.com/mycompany/lib => ../local/lib
```

> **Key Point:** Since Go 1.16, `GOPATH` mode is deprecated. All projects should use modules. `go mod tidy` should be run after any dependency change.

quiz.question: What command initializes a new Go module?
What does go mod tidy do?
